title: fees.rs has two coin-selection implementations (select_notes + select_notes_spendable)
description: fees.rs exports select_notes operating on &[Note], while tx_builder.rs has select_notes_spendable operating on &[SpendableNote]. The two functions implement the same FIFO coin-selection algorithm independently. They diverge only in the input type and error type (CoinSelectError vs TxBuildError). Both are tested separately. A contributor extending the selection strategy (e.g. adding privacy-set maximization) would need to update both. Suggested rewrite: make select_notes generic over a trait NoteValue { fn value_zatoshi(&self) -> u64; fn block_height(&self) -> u64; } and implement it for both Note and SpendableNote. Or: delete select_notes from fees.rs (it is not called from the payment path) and keep only select_notes_spendable.
close_reason: Opinion/elegance: the two implementations operate on different types (Note vs SpendableNote) and are tested independently. Neither is on a hot path. Unifying requires a trait abstraction for one consumer — premature for now.
